note: Every record quotes official text verbatim, and src/legal_time/deadlines.py refuses to load a record whose quote is not found in the chunk it cites. That check stops a fabricated deadline, it does not make these records legally correct: a human reviewer must still confirm that each period applies to the situation the app will show it for, and that no amendment or proviso changes it. Until review_status is 'reviewed', the UI labels these as unverified.

deadlines.title: Time limit to file a consumer complaint
deadlines.runs_from: the date on which the cause of action has arisen
deadlines.consequence: A consumer commission shall not admit the complaint. The Act allows a complaint to be entertained later only if the complainant satisfies the commission that there was sufficient cause for the delay.
deadlines.quote: shall not admit a complaint unless it is filed within two years from the date on which the cause of action has arisen

deadlines.title: Time limit to apply to the authority about unpaid wages
deadlines.runs_from: the date on which the claim arises
deadlines.consequence: The application is out of time. The Code allows the authority to entertain it after three years only if the applicant shows sufficient cause for the delay.
deadlines.quote: may be filed within a period of three years from the date on which claims referred to in sub

deadlines.title: When monthly wages must be paid
deadlines.runs_from: the end of the wage month
deadlines.consequence: Wages for a monthly wage period must be paid before the expiry of the seventh day of the succeeding month. Payment after that is late under the Code.
deadlines.quote: before the expiry of the seventh day of the succeeding month
